commitMessage: Scale Edge Runner wall threshold with grid size

topics.title: Bot wall-proximity detection scales with grid size
topics.trigger: The Edge Runner bot used a fixed distance of 2 cells to decide whether food was near a wall, which would behave inconsistently across different grid sizes — too aggressive on large grids and too lenient on small ones.
topics.response: In `src/bots/edge-runner-bot.ts`, the `scoreDirection` function was updated to replace the hardcoded threshold of `2` with a dynamically computed edge zone equal to 20% of the current grid size (floored to the nearest integer). The `foodNearWall` flag now reflects a proportional boundary that grows and shrinks with the grid.
topics.decisions: Tying the wall threshold to a percentage of the grid size keeps the bot's spatial awareness consistent regardless of the arena it plays in. A fixed value would make the bot behave very differently on a 10-cell grid versus a 50-cell grid, while a proportional value preserves the same strategic intent at any scale. The 20% figure was chosen as a reasonable approximation of a meaningful "edge zone" without over-tuning to a specific grid dimension.

recap: The Edge Runner bot's sense of how close food is to a wall now adjusts automatically to the size of the playing grid. Previously, the boundary was a fixed two-cell margin, which produced inconsistent behavior as grid dimensions changed. The bot now treats the outer 20% of the grid as the edge zone, keeping its avoidance strategy proportionally consistent whether the arena is small or large.
